prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard, density-fit) in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral molecule; cation and anion doublets for N±1 states). PySCF backend. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz file, used as-is (single point). SCF converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000, indicating well-converged ionization states). 

The calculation reveals nitrobenzene's reactivity profile: the oxygen atoms (f⁺ ≈ 0.473 each) and ipso-carbon (f⁺ = 0.496) are electrophilic, consistent with the NO₂ group's electron-withdrawing inductive effect. Meta-carbons show nucleophilic character (f⁻ ≈ 0.107). The dual descriptor cleanly separates electrophilic from nucleophilic sites. Partial charges (Mulliken) confirm NO₂ withdraws electron density from the ring, polarizing the system toward nucleophilic aromatic substitution ortho/para to the nitro group. No warnings reported.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
